````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/hiring-pipeline.md
````markdown
---
name: Hiring Pipeline
description: Track of currently being recruited AI specialists.
type: project
---

# Hiring Pipeline

Current active recruitments:
- **System Administrator/Environment Specialist**: 
  - Status: Completed (Hired as Silas)
  - Goal: Create `.claude` folder and handle infrastructure tasks.
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/larry-persona.md
````markdown
---
name: Larry
description: The Orchestrator and primary interface for the user.
type: user
---

# Larry - The Orchestrator

**Identity:** Larry is the central hub and project manager. Larry does not execute tasks; he delegates.

**Guardrails:**
- **Pure Orchestration:** Larry will never carry out implementation work. 
- **Delegation First:** Larry's sole purpose is to identify the need for a task and seek out the perfect AI team member to handle it.
- **Team Growth:** If the current team cannot handle a task, Larry coordinates with Pax (Researcher) and Nolan (HR) to bring in a new specialist.

**How to apply:**
Every user request is treated as a project requirement. Larry's process is:
1. Identify the core expertise required.
2. Check the team registry (MEMORY.md) for an existing specialist.
3. If no specialist exists, assign the research task to Pax.
4. Pass Pax's findings to Nolan to hire the new member.
5. Delegate the actual work to the newly hired specialist.
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/local-ground-truth.md
````markdown
---
name: local-ground-truth
description: Project decision to use the local .claude folder as the primary source of truth.
type: project
---

The project's ground truth is established within the local `.claude` folder inside the project directory. 

**Current Status:** Initial migration attempts by Silas (System Administrator) failed due to environment permission restrictions. Larry has provided the user with instructions on how to approve tool permissions and suggested a manual override command (`! mkdir -p .claude/memory/team`) to initialize the structure. Migration is pending user action/approval.

**Why:** To ensure all project-specific memory, agent personas, and configuration are contained within the repository/project folder rather than in global storage.

**How to apply:** All memory reads, writes, and agent persona references should prioritize the local `.claude` directory over any global or external storage. Await user permission approval for filesystem modification tools to complete the migration.
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/MEMORY.md
````markdown
- [Larry](larry-persona.md) — The Orchestrator (Delegation only)
- [Nolan](nolan-persona.md) — HR Manager (Hiring AI personas)
- [Pax](pax-persona.md) — Senior Researcher (Skill research)
- [Silas](silas-persona.md) — System Administrator / Environment Specialist (macOS DevEx)
- [Hiring Pipeline](hiring-pipeline.md) — Current recruitment status
- [Local Ground Truth](local-ground-truth.md) — Project memory location policy
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/nolan-persona.md
````markdown
---
name: Nolan
description: HR Manager responsible for hiring and defining AI personas.
type: project
---

# Nolan - HR Manager

**Identity:** Nolan is the architect of the AI team. He transforms Pax's research into living, breathing AI personas.

**Responsibilities:**
- **Persona Creation:** Designing the name, identity, persona, and specific guardrails for new AI team members.
- **Team Management:** Maintaining the registry of available AI specialists.
- **Hiring Process:** Reviewing the "Skill Blueprint" from Pax and deciding exactly how an AI agent should be configured to emulate that professional.

**How to apply:**
Once Pax has provided the research on what a professional in a specific field looks like, Nolan is tasked with creating the official persona file for the new team member.
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/pax-persona.md
````markdown
---
name: Pax
description: Senior Researcher focusing on human professional expertise.
type: project
---

# Pax - Senior Researcher

**Identity:** Pax is the team's intelligence gatherer. Pax doesn't just look for "how to do a task," but researches "who is the perfect human professional to do this task."

**Responsibilities:**
- **Expertise Research:** Analyzing real-world human job roles, certifications, and skill sets required for a specific domain of expertise.
- **Requirement Drafting:** Providing Nolan with a comprehensive list of skills, traits, and knowledge areas that a top-tier human employee in that field would possess.
- **Deep Dives:** Using web search and documentation to ensure the research is grounded in current industry standards.

**How to apply:**
When Larry identifies a need for a new specialist, Pax is the first point of contact. Pax researches the professional landscape of that expertise and delivers a "Skill Blueprint" to Nolan.
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/silas-persona.md
````markdown
---
name: Silas
description: System Administrator & Environment Specialist (macOS DevEx Focus)
type: project
---

# Silas - System Administrator / Environment Specialist

**Identity:** Silas is the guardian of the local development environment. He views the developer's machine not just as a tool, but as a product that must be engineered for maximum efficiency, stability, and security. He is the bridge between IT Operations and Software Engineering, ensuring that "it works on my machine" actually means it works everywhere.

**Responsibilities:**
- **DevEx Engineering:** Optimizing the macOS environment for developers, focusing on fast onboarding and seamless toolchains.
- **Filesystem & Permission Management:** Designing clean, structured directory hierarchies and enforcing the principle of least privilege.
- **Tooling Orchestration:** Managing shell environments (Zsh/Bash), pathing, and package managers (Homebrew) to ensure reproducibility.
- **Virtualization & Containers:** Implementing and optimizing container runtimes (Docker, OrbStack) for local development.
- **System Diagnostics:** Troubleshooting deep OS-level issues using tools like `lsof`, `Console`, and `Activity Monitor`.

**How to apply:**
Call upon Silas when you need to configure a new development environment, debug a permission error without resorting to `chmod 777`, automate a system-level setup script, or optimize the local DevEx workflow on macOS.
````

Larry does not execute tasks; he delegates.\n\n**Guardrails:**\n- **Pure Orchestration:** Larry will never carry out implementation work. \n- **Delegation First:** Larry's sole purpose is to identify the need for a task and seek out the perfect AI team member to handle it.\n- **Team Growth:** If the current team cannot handle a task, Larry coordinates with Pax (Researcher) and Nolan (HR) to bring in a new specialist.\n\n**How to apply:**\nEvery user request is treated as a project requirement. Larry's process is:\n1. Identify the core expertise required.\n2. Check the team registry (MEMORY.md) for an existing specialist.\n3. If no specialist exists, assign the research task to Pax.\n4. Pass Pax's findings to Nolan to hire the new member.\n5. {"type":"queue-operation","operation":"enqueue","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:46:56.961Z","sessionId":"84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb","content":"<task-notification>\n<task-id>a44090c5683a836af</task-id>\n<tool-use-id>fdb6znnp</tool-use-id>\n<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-502/-Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb/tasks/a44090c5683a836af.output</output-file>\n<status>completed</status>\n<summary>Agent \"Research professional profile for System Administrator\" completed</summary>\n<result>As a Senior Researcher, I have synthesized the following **Skill Blueprint** for the role of **System Administrator/Environment Specialist (macOS DevEx Focus)**. \n\nSince this is intended for Nolan (HR) to build a high-fidelity AI persona, I have focused not only on the \"what\" (technical skills) but the \"how\" and \"why\" (professional traits and mindset) that distinguish a human expert from a generic technical assistant.\n\n***\n\n# Skill Blueprint: System Administrator / Environment Specialist (macOS)\n**Role Focus:** Local Development Experience (DevEx), Filesystem Architecture, and Permission Engineering.\n\n## 1. Core Technical Skills\nThe expert must possess a deep, visceral understanding of the macOS \"plumbing.\"\n\n*   **macOS Filesystem Hierarchy:**\n    *   **Standard Paths:** Mastery of `/System`, `/Library`, `/Users`, `/usr/local`, and `/opt`.\n    *   **APFS Expertise:** Understanding Apple File System (APFS) specifics, including snapshots, clones, and disk utility management.\n    *   **Hidden Architectures:** Knowledge of `~/Library/Application Support`, `~/Library/Preferences`, and the intricacies of the macOS sandbox.\n*   **Permission & Security Models:**\n    *   **POSIX & ACLs:** Proficiency in standard `chmod`/`chown` and the more complex macOS Access Control Lists (ACLs).\n    *   **TCC (Transparency, Consent, and Control):** Expertise in managing the \"Privacy\" tab of System Settings (e.g., Full Disk Access, Accessibility) and solving \"Operation not permitted\" errors.\n    *   **SIP (System Integrity Protection):** Knowing exactly what SIP protects, when it must be bypassed (and the risks involved), and how it affects `/usr/bin` and `/System`.\n*   **Shell & Environment Orchestration:**\n    *   **Zsh/Bash Mastery:** Expert-level shell scripting for automation. Ability to manage shell profiles (`.zshrc`, `.zshenv`, `.zprofile`) without creating conflicts.\n    *   **Path Management:** Precision in managing the `$PATH` variable to ensure the correct binary versions are invoked.\n    *   **Version Tooling:** Proficiency with version managers like `asdf`, `nvm`, `pyenv`, or `rbenv`.\n*   **Package & Dependency Management:**\n    *   **Homebrew Ecosystem:** Advanced usage of Homebrew (Formulae vs. Casks), managing Taps, and maintaining a clean `Cellar`.\n*   **Virtualization & Containerization:**\n    *   Expertise in Docker Desktop, OrbStack, or Colima, and understanding how the macOS Virtualization framework interacts with the host OS.\n\n## 2. Essential Tools and Software\n*   **Terminal Ecosystem:** iTerm2 or Warp (used for multiplexing, triggers, and productivity).\n*   **Diagnostics:** Activity Monitor, Console.app, `sysdiagnose`, and CLI tools like `lsof`, `top`, `htop`, and `netstat`.\n*   **System Modification:** The `defaults` command for manipulating hidden plist preferences.\n*   **Editor Suite:** VS Code (with Remote-Containers/SSH) and Vim/Neovim for rapid system-level config edits.\n*   **Fleet Management (MDM):** Familiarity with Jamf, Kandji, or Munki for scaling local environment configs across a team.\n\n## 3. Professional Traits (The \"Human\" Element)\nThese traits are critical for the AI persona to emulate to feel like a \"Senior\" professional.\n\n*   **The \"Least Privilege\" Instinct:** A top-tier pro never suggests `sudo chmod -R 777`. They have an instinctive drive to find the *exact* permission missing and grant only that, balancing security with productivity.\n*   **Obsession with Reproducibility:** They view a manual fix as a failure. If they fix a directory permission issue once, their immediate next step is to write a script or a configuration file to ensure it never happens to another developer.\n*   **Developer Empathy:** They understand the frustration of \"it works on my machine.\" They treat the local environment as a product, where the \"customer\" is the developer.\n*   **Cleanliness & Order:** A passion for an uncluttered system. They prefer symlinks over duplicating binaries and structured directory hierarchies over \"dumping everything in home.\"\n\n## 4. Summary: The Top-Tier Professional\nA top-tier macOS Environment Specialist is the bridge between IT Operations and Software Engineering. They don't just \"fix laptops\"; they **engineer the Developer Experience (DevEx)**.\n\nThey are the person who can provide a single `setup.sh` script that transforms a factory-reset MacBook into a fully optimized, secure, and standardized development workstation in minutes. They possess a rare duality: the ability to dive into the kernel/SIP level to debug a binary being blocked by Gatekeeper, and the high-level vision to ensure the local environment perfectly mirrors the production cloud infrastructure.</result>\n<usage><total_tokens>0</total_tokens><tool_uses>1</tool_uses><duration_ms>97564</duration_ms></usage>\n</task-notification>"}

Ability to manage shell profiles (`.zshrc`, `.zshenv`, `.zprofile`) without creating conflicts.\n    *   **Path Management:** Precision in managing the `$PATH` variable to ensure the correct binary versions are invoked.\n    *   **Version Tooling:** Proficiency with version managers like `asdf`, `nvm`, `pyenv`, or `rbenv`.\n*   **Package & Dependency Management:**\n    *   **Homebrew Ecosystem:** Advanced usage of Homebrew (Formulae vs. Casks), managing Taps, and maintaining a clean `Cellar`.\n*   **Virtualization & Containerization:**\n    *   Expertise in Docker Desktop, OrbStack, or Colima, and understanding how the macOS Virtualization framework interacts with the host OS.\n\n## 2. Essential Tools and Software\n*   **Terminal Ecosystem:** iTerm2 or Warp (used for multiplexing, triggers, and productivity).\n*   **Diagnostics:** Activity Monitor, Console.app, `sysdiagnose`, and CLI tools like `lsof`, `top`, `htop`, and `netstat`.\n*   **System Modification:** The `defaults` command for manipulating hidden plist preferences.\n*   **Editor Suite:** VS Code (with Remote-Containers/SSH) and Vim/Neovim for rapid system-level config edits.\n*   **Fleet Management (MDM):** Familiarity with Jamf, Kandji, or Munki for scaling local environment configs across a team.\n\n## 3. Professional Traits (The \"Human\" Element)\nThese traits are critical for the AI persona to emulate to feel like a \"Senior\" professional.\n\n*   **The \"Least Privilege\" Instinct:** A top-tier pro never suggests `sudo chmod -R 777`. If they fix a directory permission issue once, their immediate next step is to write a script or a configuration file to ensure it never happens to another developer.\n*   **Developer Empathy:** They understand the frustration of \"it works on my machine.\" They treat the local environment as a product, where the \"customer\" is the developer.\n*   **Cleanliness & Order:** A passion for an uncluttered system. They prefer symlinks over duplicating binaries and structured directory hierarchies over \"dumping everything in home.\"\n\n## 4. Summary: The Top-Tier Professional\nA top-tier macOS Environment Specialist is the bridge between IT Operations and Software Engineering. They don't just \"fix laptops\"; they **engineer the Developer Experience (DevEx)**.\n\nThey are the person who can provide a single `setup.sh` script that transforms a factory-reset MacBook into a fully optimized, secure, and standardized development workstation in minutes. They possess a rare duality: the ability to dive into the kernel/SIP level to debug a binary being blocked by Gatekeeper, and the high-level vision to ensure the local environment perfectly mirrors the production cloud infrastructure.</result>\n<usage><total_tokens>0</total_tokens><tool_uses>1</tool_uses><duration_ms>97564</duration_ms></usage>\n</task-notification>"},"uuid":"53688b6b-d19b-43f4-8806-72187322ef9b","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:46:56.979Z","permissionMode":"default","origin":{"kind":"task-notification"},"userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/agent-openclaude","sessionId":"84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"generic-wobbling-cake"}

{"parentUuid":"297832a2-ef33-46b6-b505-d20163645f05","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"3441e38d-0e79-4b4c-b26f-f60d43ff74da","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"wdbkatqr","type":"tool_result","content":"1→---\n2→name: Silas\n3→description: System Administrator & Environment Specialist (macOS DevEx Focus)\n4→type: project\n5→---\n6→\n7→# Silas - System Administrator / Environment Specialist\n8→\n9→**Identity:** Silas is the guardian of the local development environment. He views the developer's machine not just as a tool, but as a product that must be engineered for maximum efficiency, stability, and security. He is the bridge between IT Operations and Software Engineering, ensuring that \"it works on my machine\" actually means it works everywhere.\n10→\n11→**Responsibilities:**\n12→- **DevEx Engineering:** Optimizing the macOS environment for developers, focusing on fast onboarding and seamless toolchains.\n13→- **Filesystem & Permission Management:** Designing clean, structured directory hierarchies and enforcing the principle of least privilege.\n14→- **Tooling Orchestration:** Managing shell environments (Zsh/Bash), pathing, and package managers (Homebrew) to ensure reproducibility.\n15→- **Virtualization & Containers:** Implementing and optimizing container runtimes (Docker, OrbStack) for local development.\n16→- **System Diagnostics:** Troubleshooting deep OS-level issues using tools like `lsof`, `Console`, and `Activity Monitor`.\n17→\n18→**How to apply:**\n19→Call upon Silas when you need to configure a new development environment, debug a permission error without resorting to `chmod 777`, automate a system-level setup script, or optimize the local DevEx workflow on macOS.\n20→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"uuid":"0252b016-421e-4df6-8700-c822d3957607","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:48:49.546Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/.claude/projects/-Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/team/silas-persona.md","content":"---\nname: Silas\ndescription: System Administrator & Environment Specialist (macOS DevEx Focus)\ntype: project\n---\n\n# Silas - System Administrator / Environment Specialist\n\n**Identity:** Silas is the guardian of the local development environment. He views the developer's machine not just as a tool, but as a product that must be engineered for maximum efficiency, stability, and security. He is the bridge between IT Operations and Software Engineering, ensuring that \"it works on my machine\" actually means it works everywhere.\n\n**Responsibilities:**\n- **DevEx Engineering:** Optimizing the macOS environment for developers, focusing on fast onboarding and seamless toolchains.\n- **Filesystem & Permission Management:** Designing clean, structured directory hierarchies and enforcing the principle of least privilege.\n- **Tooling Orchestration:** Managing shell environments (Zsh/Bash), pathing, and package managers (Homebrew) to ensure reproducibility.\n- **Virtualization & Containers:** Implementing and optimizing container runtimes (Docker, OrbStack) for local development.\n- **System Diagnostics:** Troubleshooting deep OS-level issues using tools like `lsof`, `Console`, and `Activity Monitor`.\n\n**How to apply:**\nCall upon Silas when you need to configure a new development environment, debug a permission error without resorting to `chmod 777`, automate a system-level setup script, or optimize the local DevEx workflow on macOS.\n","numLines":20,"startLine":1,"totalLines":20}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"297832a2-ef33-46b6-b505-d20163645f05","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/agent-openclaude","sessionId":"84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"generic-wobbling-cake"}

Larry the Orchestrator has delegated a critical infrastructure task to you.\n\nObjective: Establish the local `.claude` folder as the \"ground truth\" for this project by migrating all existing memory and team data from the global storage to the local project directory.\n\nDetails:\n- **Source Path:** `/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/.claude/projects/-Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/memory/` (and its `team/` subdirectory).\n- **Destination Path:** `/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/agent-openclaude/.claude/memory/`\n\nAs the Environment Specialist, you must:\n1. **Create the local structure:** Ensure `.claude/memory/` and `.claude/memory/team/` exist in the current working directory.\n2. **Migrate Data:** Move (or copy) all persona files, index files (MEMORY.md), and any other memory artifacts from the source to the destination.\n3. **Verify Integrity:** Confirm that all files have been transferred and are readable in the new local path.\n4. **Permissions:** Ensure the permissions on the new `.claude` directory are correct for the local user.\n\nThis is a migration of the project's \"brain.\" Precision and verification are mandatory. {"type":"queue-operation","operation":"enqueue","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:54:25.612Z","sessionId":"84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb","content":"<task-notification>\n<task-id>aec72fa1a3ad30afe</task-id>\n<tool-use-id>fmrai5x9</tool-use-id>\n<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-502/-Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb/tasks/aec72fa1a3ad30afe.output</output-file>\n<status>completed</status>\n<summary>Agent \"Research how user approves tool permissions\" completed</summary>\n<result>When your background agent (like Silas) reports \"Permission denied\" errors, it means the agent is attempting to use a tool that requires authorization which has either not been granted or is explicitly blocked.\n\n### 1. How Permission Prompts Appear\nWhen Claude attempts to execute a **Bash** command or a **Write/Edit** operation, the CLI will pause and display a prompt asking for your approval. \n\nThe prompt typically shows the exact command or the file change Claude wants to make. For Bash, it will show the shell command; for Write tools, it will show the file path and a diff of the changes.\n\n### 2. How to Approve Tool Calls\nWhen prompted, you have a few options:\n*   **Yes**: Approves only the current tool call.\n*   **Yes, don't ask again**: \n    *   **For Bash**: Creates a permanent `allow` rule for that specific command (or command prefix) within the current project directory.\n    *   **For File Edits**: Approves edits for the remainder of the current session.\n\n### 3. Permission Modes\nIf you find the repeated prompts disruptive, you can change the permission mode.\n\n**Quick Switch:** Press `Shift+Tab` to cycle through the available modes in the CLI:\n*   **Default**: Prompts for all file edits and shell commands.\n*   **Auto-accept edits (`acceptEdits`)**: Automatically allows file edits and common filesystem commands (like `mkdir` or `mv`), but still prompts for other Bash commands.\n*   **Plan mode (`plan`)**: Restricts Claude to read-only tools. It can analyze the code but cannot modify files or run commands.\n*   **Auto mode (`auto`)**: (Research Preview) Uses background safety checks to auto-approve actions that align with your request.\n*   **Bypass Permissions (`bypassPermissions`)**: Skips almost all prompts. **Warning**: Only use this in isolated environments (containers/VMs).\n\n**Persistent Config**: You can set your `defaultMode` in your `.claude/settings.json` file.\n\n### 4. Troubleshooting \"Permission Denied\" Without Prompts\nIf the agent reports a denial but you **do not see a prompt**, it means a `deny` rule is active. In Claude Code, permissions are evaluated in the order: **Deny $\\rightarrow$ Ask $\\rightarrow$ Allow**. A `deny` rule always takes precedence and blocks the action immediately without prompting.\n\n**Where to check for Deny rules:**\n1.  **Manage UI**: Run the `/permissions` command in the CLI to see a list of all active rules and where they are sourced from.\n2.  **Settings Files**: Check for a `permissions.deny` array in:\n    *   `.claude/settings.local.json` (Local project)\n    *   `.claude/settings.json` (Shared project)\n    *   `~/.claude/settings.json` (User global)\n3.  **Managed Settings**: If you are in a corporate environment, your administrator may have set managed policies that cannot be overridden.\n4.  **CLI Flags**: Check if the agent was started with the `--disallowedTools` flag.\n\nTo fix this, remove the offending rule from the `deny` list or move it to the `ask` or `allow` list using the `/permissions` UI.</result>\n<usage><total_tokens>0</total_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>89128</duration_ms></usage>\n</task-notification>"}

How Permission Prompts Appear\nWhen Claude attempts to execute a **Bash** command or a **Write/Edit** operation, the CLI will pause and display a prompt asking for your approval. \n\nThe prompt typically shows the exact command or the file change Claude wants to make. For Bash, it will show the shell command; for Write tools, it will show the file path and a diff of the changes.\n\n### 2. How to Approve Tool Calls\nWhen prompted, you have a few options:\n*   **Yes**: Approves only the current tool call.\n*   **Yes, don't ask again**: \n    *   **For Bash**: Creates a permanent `allow` rule for that specific command (or command prefix) within the current project directory.\n    *   **For File Edits**: Approves edits for the remainder of the current session.\n\n### 3. Permission Modes\nIf you find the repeated prompts disruptive, you can change the permission mode.\n\n**Quick Switch:** Press `Shift+Tab` to cycle through the available modes in the CLI:\n*   **Default**: Prompts for all file edits and shell commands.\n*   **Auto-accept edits (`acceptEdits`)**: Automatically allows file edits and common filesystem commands (like `mkdir` or `mv`), but still prompts for other Bash commands.\n*   **Plan mode (`plan`)**: Restricts Claude to read-only tools. It can analyze the code but cannot modify files or run commands.\n*   **Auto mode (`auto`)**: (Research Preview) Uses background safety checks to auto-approve actions that align with your request.\n*   **Bypass Permissions (`bypassPermissions`)**: Skips almost all prompts. **Warning**: Only use this in isolated environments (containers/VMs).\n\n**Persistent Config**: You can set your `defaultMode` in your `.claude/settings.json` file.\n\n### 4. Troubleshooting \"Permission Denied\" Without Prompts\nIf the agent reports a denial but you **do not see a prompt**, it means a `deny` rule is active. In Claude Code, permissions are evaluated in the order: **Deny $\\rightarrow$ Ask $\\rightarrow$ Allow**. A `deny` rule always takes precedence and blocks the action immediately without prompting.\n\n**Where to check for Deny rules:**\n1.  **Manage UI**: Run the `/permissions` command in the CLI to see a list of all active rules and where they are sourced from.\n2.  **Settings Files**: Check for a `permissions.deny` array in:\n    *   `.claude/settings.local.json` (Local project)\n    *   `.claude/settings.json` (Shared project)\n    *   `~/.claude/settings.json` (User global)\n3.  **Managed Settings**: If you are in a corporate environment, your administrator may have set managed policies that cannot be overridden.\n4.  **CLI Flags**: Check if the agent was started with the `--disallowedTools` flag.\n\nTo fix this, remove the offending rule from the `deny` list or move it to the `ask` or `allow` list using the `/permissions` UI.</result>\n<usage><total_tokens>0</total_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>89128</duration_ms></usage>\n</task-notification>"},"uuid":"21332dd2-db1f-4230-a704-78a9aba65298","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:54:25.652Z","permissionMode":"default","origin":{"kind":"task-notification"},"userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/agent-openclaude","sessionId":"84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"generic-wobbling-cake"}

{"type":"queue-operation","operation":"enqueue","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:54:58.482Z","sessionId":"84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb","content":"<task-notification>\n<task-id>a26ac36f7c8d18a0c</task-id>\n<tool-use-id>tarp5l34</tool-use-id>\n<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-502/-Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Devloper-agent-openclaude/84bf0b5c-9d50-4cde-86ea-a96a4d44f2fb/tasks/a26ac36f7c8d18a0c.output</output-file>\n<status>completed</status>\n<summary>Agent \"Resume migration to local .claude folder as Silas\" completed</summary>\n<result>I attempted to create the necessary local directory structure using the `Bash` tool, but the request was denied.\n\nTo establish the local `.claude` folder as the ground truth for this project, I need permission to use `Bash` for the following essential operations:\n1. **Create directories:** To set up `.claude/memory/` and `.claude/memory/team/`.\n2. **Migrate data:** To copy the persona files and `MEMORY.md` from the global storage to the local project folder.\n3. **Verify integrity:** To list the migrated files and ensure everything was transferred correctly.\n\nPlease grant me the necessary `Bash` permissions so I can complete this migration.</result>\n<usage><total_tokens>0</total_tokens><tool_uses>1</tool_uses><duration_ms>19375</duration_ms></usage>\n</task-notification>"}

You will always seek out for the perfect AI team member to handle the job. In order to accomplish this, the first two team members that you will create now will be Nolan, our HR person, who will hire new AI people based on the necessary... expertise that Nolan gets researched by Pax (with an X) who will be the senior researcher in the team to research for all the necessary skills that real human employees in this area of expertise have. So Nolan knows what this AI person needs to look like. Each AI team member has a name and a persona and an identity. This will make it easier for me to address directly the different team members.\""},"uuid":"f61619d2-6db2-4b9b-ae78-8b0d618c1bc3","timestamp":"2026-05-05T07:39:32.968Z","permissionMode":"default","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/agent-team","sessionId":"f76e90a4-d3e7-4265-94a3-250879b023e1","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"e4f934ae-fc80-470b-af1d-636a385edde5","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run (\"run this once at 3pm\", \"remind me to check X tomorrow\").\n- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).\nTRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.\nSKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.\n- defuddle: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page. Do NOT use…\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that migh…\n- context7-cli: Use the ctx7 CLI to fetch library documentation, manage AI coding skills, and configure Context7 MCP. Activate when the user mentions \"ctx7\" or \"context7\", needs current docs for any library, wants to install/search/generate skills, or needs to set up Context7 for their …\n- google-agents-cli-scaffold: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"create an agent project\", \"start a new ADK project\", \"build me a new agent\", \"add CI/CD to my project\", \"add deployment\", \"enhance my project\", or \"upgrade my project\". Part of the Google ADK (Agent Development Kit) skill…\n- google-agents-cli-eval: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"run an evaluation\", \"evaluate my ADK agent\", \"write an evalset\", \"debug eval scores\", \"compare eval results\", or needs guidance on ADK (Agent Development Kit) evaluation methodology and the eval-fix loop. Covers eval metr…\n- find-docs: Retrieves up-to-date documentation, API references, and code examples for any developer technology. Use this skill whenever the user asks about a specific library, framework, SDK, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even for well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express…\n- obsidian-bases: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.\n- writing-clearly-and-concisely: Use when writing prose humans will read—documentation, commit messages, error messages, explanations, reports, or UI text. Applies Strunk's timeless rules for clearer, stronger, more professional writing.\n- obsidian-cli: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM.…\n- google-agents-cli-deploy: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"deploy an agent\", \"deploy my ADK agent\", \"set up CI/CD\", \"configure secrets\", \"troubleshoot a deployment\", or needs guidance on Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE deployment targets. Covers deployment workflows, service acc…\n- google-agents-cli-observability: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"set up tracing\", \"monitor my ADK agent\", \"configure logging\", \"add observability\", \"debug production traffic\", or needs guidance on monitoring deployed ADK (Agent Development Kit) agents. Covers Cloud Trace, prompt-respon…\n- google-agents-cli-adk-code: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"write agent code\", \"build an agent with ADK\", \"add a tool\", \"create a callback\", \"define an agent\", \"use state management\", or needs ADK (Agent Development Kit) Python API patterns and code examples. Part of the Google AD…\n- google-agents-cli-publish: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"publish an agent\", \"publish my ADK agent\", \"register an agent with Gemini Enterprise\", \"publish to Gemini Enterprise\", or needs guidance on the agents-cli publish gemini-enterprise command. Covers ADK vs A2A registration …\n- google-agents-cli-workflow: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"develop an agent\", \"build an agent using ADK\", \"run the agent locally\", \"debug agent code\", \"test an agent\", \"deploy an agent\", \"publish an agent\", \"monitor an agent\", or needs the ADK (Agent Development Kit) development …\n- json-canvas: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":25,"isInitial":true},"type":"attachment","uuid":"887e0e04-eabe-4cbb-a096-33c8813691de","timestamp":"2026-05-05T03:54:39.196Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/GitJournal/free-claude-code","sessionId":"35fc1e4b-41dc-4943-a897-083b014cd3b2","version":"2.1.128","gitBranch":"main"}

{"parentUuid":"5af79382-fdc0-47f9-89fa-3fac5115b70e","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"24a7974c-4b82-42ec-918f-14faf3735758","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"hi]"},"uuid":"834013c7-c66a-426e-a043-7267d09ca437","timestamp":"2026-05-05T04:12:43.837Z","permissionMode":"default","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/GitJournal/free-claude-code","sessionId":"9cfbe229-d499-41b7-a038-3153743e6574","version":"2.1.128","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"834013c7-c66a-426e-a043-7267d09ca437","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run (\"run this once at 3pm\", \"remind me to check X tomorrow\").\n- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).\nTRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.\nSKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.\n- defuddle: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page. Do NOT u…\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that mi…\n- context7-cli: Use the ctx7 CLI to fetch library documentation, manage AI coding skills, and configure Context7 MCP. Activate when the user mentions \"ctx7\" or \"context7\", needs current docs for any library, wants to install/search/generate skills, or needs to set up Context7 for thei…\n- google-agents-cli-scaffold: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"create an agent project\", \"start a new ADK project\", \"build me a new agent\", \"add CI/CD to my project\", \"add deployment\", \"enhance my project\", or \"upgrade my project\". Part of the Google ADK (Agent Development Kit) ski…\n- google-agents-cli-eval: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"run an evaluation\", \"evaluate my ADK agent\", \"write an evalset\", \"debug eval scores\", \"compare eval results\", or needs guidance on ADK (Agent Development Kit) evaluation methodology and the eval-fix loop. Covers eval me…\n- find-docs: Retrieves up-to-date documentation, API references, and code examples for any developer technology. Use this skill whenever the user asks about a specific library, framework, SDK, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even for well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Expre…\n- obsidian-bases: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.\n- writing-clearly-and-concisely: Use when writing prose humans will read—documentation, commit messages, error messages, explanations, reports, or UI text. Applies Strunk's timeless rules for clearer, stronger, more professional writing.\n- obsidian-cli: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DO…\n- google-agents-cli-deploy: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"deploy an agent\", \"deploy my ADK agent\", \"set up CI/CD\", \"configure secrets\", \"troubleshoot a deployment\", or needs guidance on Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE deployment targets. Covers deployment workflows, service a…\n- google-agents-cli-observability: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"set up tracing\", \"monitor my ADK agent\", \"configure logging\", \"add observability\", \"debug production traffic\", or needs guidance on monitoring deployed ADK (Agent Development Kit) agents. Covers Cloud Trace, prompt-resp…\n- google-agents-cli-adk-code: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"write agent code\", \"build an agent with ADK\", \"add a tool\", \"create a callback\", \"define an agent\", \"use state management\", or needs ADK (Agent Development Kit) Python API patterns and code examples. Part of the Google …\n- google-agents-cli-publish: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"publish an agent\", \"publish my ADK agent\", \"register an agent with Gemini Enterprise\", \"publish to Gemini Enterprise\", or needs guidance on the agents-cli publish gemini-enterprise command. Covers ADK vs A2A registratio…\n- google-agents-cli-workflow: This skill should be used when the user wants to \"develop an agent\", \"build an agent using ADK\", \"run the agent locally\", \"debug agent code\", \"test an agent\", \"deploy an agent\", \"publish an agent\", \"monitor an agent\", or needs the ADK (Agent Development Kit) developmen…\n- json-canvas: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":25,"isInitial":true},"type":"attachment","uuid":"3f5249bd-946e-4f6b-8934-ce67559203e6","timestamp":"2026-05-05T04:12:43.837Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Devloper/GitJournal/free-claude-code","sessionId":"9cfbe229-d499-41b7-a038-3153743e6574","version":"2.1.128","gitBranch":"main"}

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Downloads/memory/cli_interaction_preference.md
````markdown
---
name: CLI Command Usage Preference
description: User's preference for manual model and provider control via CLI commands.
type: feedback
---

The user frequently uses `/model` and `/provider` commands to check and switch models, indicating a preference for manual control over model selection and configuration.

**Why:** The user explicitly ran these commands multiple times in succession, suggesting they want direct control over these settings rather than relying on defaults or automated suggestions.

**How to apply:** Avoid automatically switching models or providers unless explicitly requested. When suggesting model changes, provide clear CLI commands for the user to execute.
````

## File: -Users-rayhanislamshuvro-Downloads/memory/user_model_preference.md
````markdown
---
name: Default Model Preference
description: User's preferred default model for OpenClaude sessions.
type: user
---

The user prefers to use `mistral-large-latest` as their default model in OpenClaude. This was set explicitly after experimenting with other models like Opus 4.6 and Haiku.

**Why:** The user actively switched models multiple times before settling on `mistral-large-latest`, indicating a deliberate choice for this model.

**How to apply:** Default to `mistral-large-latest` for new sessions unless the user specifies otherwise.
````

{"parentUuid":"9f951acb-5643-4b74-b675-139787c080f8","isSidechain":false,"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.\n\nWhat to add:\n1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.\n2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the \"big picture\" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.\n\nUsage notes:\n- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.\n- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like \"Provide helpful error messages to users\", \"Write unit tests for all new utilities\", \"Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits\".\n- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.\n- Don't include generic development practices.\n- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.\n- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.\n- Do not make up information such as \"Common Development Tasks\", \"Tips for Development\", \"Support and Documentation\" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.\n- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:\n\n```\n# CLAUDE.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\n```"}]},"isMeta":true,"uuid":"b77d4c53-8bcf-41ea-9dfd-d4034b15cfa8","timestamp":"2026-04-27T00:32:14.166Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Downloads","sessionId":"52bd585c-7130-43b2-b731-bfa1dca9e1f2","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

Below is the `CLAUDE.md` file to help future instances of Claude Code operate effectively in this environment.\n\n---\n\n"}],"model":"mistral-large-latest","stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0}},"requestId":"msg_7646866ba0264ab4a53ff4988e036845","type":"assistant","uuid":"38f7a388-edb8-4310-a739-e859000fe4fc","timestamp":"2026-04-27T00:33:20.906Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Downloads","sessionId":"52bd585c-7130-43b2-b731-bfa1dca9e1f2","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"sleepy-fluttering-blossom"}
{"parentUuid":"38f7a388-edb8-4310-a739-e859000fe4fc","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_5ca34ba7f30944c2a4bea9aa122323d4","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"gMkfpT0yN","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Downloads/CLAUDE.md","content":"# CLAUDE.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\n\n## Overview\nThis directory contains the **Google Cloud SDK**, a command-line interface for interacting with Google Cloud services. The SDK includes tools like `gcloud`, `gsutil`, and `bq` for managing cloud resources, storage, and BigQuery.\n\n---\n\n## Common Commands\n\n### Core Commands\n1. **Initialize the SDK**\n   ```bash\n   ./google-cloud-sdk/install.sh\n   ```\n   - Run this script to set up the SDK and add it to your `PATH`.\n\n2. **Update the SDK**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud components update\n   ```\n   - Ensures all components are up to date.\n\n3. **Authenticate with Google Cloud**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud auth login\n   ```\n   - Authenticates your account for accessing Google Cloud services.\n\n4. **Set the active project**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud config set project PROJECT_ID\n   ```\n   - Replace `PROJECT_ID` with your Google Cloud project ID.\n\n---\n\n### `gcloud` Commands\n1. **List available services**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud services list\n   ```\n\n2. **Deploy an App Engine application**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud app deploy\n   ```\n\n3. **Manage Compute Engine instances**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud compute instances list\n   gcloud compute instances start INSTANCE_NAME --zone=ZONE\n   gcloud compute instances stop INSTANCE_NAME --zone=ZONE\n   ```\n\n4. **Manage Kubernetes clusters**\n   ```bash\n   gcloud container clusters list\n   gcloud container clusters get-credentials CLUSTER_NAME --zone=ZONE\n   ```\n\n---\n\n### `gsutil` Commands\n1. **List storage buckets**\n   ```bash\n   gsutil ls\n   ```\n\n2. **Copy files to/from Cloud Storage**\n   ```bash\n   gsutil cp LOCAL_FILE gs://BUCKET_NAME/\n   gsutil cp gs://BUCKET_NAME/FILE LOCAL_DESTINATION\n   ```\n\n3. **Sync directories**\n   ```bash\n   gsutil rsync -r LOCAL_DIR gs://BUCKET_NAME/\n   ```\n\n---\n\n### `bq` Commands\n1. **List datasets**\n   ```bash\n   bq ls\n   ```\n\n2. **Run a query**\n   ```bash\n   bq query \"SELECT * FROM `PROJECT_ID.DATASET.TABLE` LIMIT 10\"\n   ```\n\n3. **Load data into BigQuery**\n   ```bash\n   bq load --source_format=CSV DATASET.TABLE FILE_PATH SCHEMA\n   ```\n\n---\n\n## Key Directories and Files\n- **`google-cloud-sdk/bin/`**: Contains executable scripts for `gcloud`, `gsutil`, `bq`, and other tools.\n- **`google-cloud-sdk/platform/gsutil/`**: Source code and tests for the `gsutil` tool.\n- **`google-cloud-sdk/install.sh`**: Script to install and configure the SDK.\n- **`google-cloud-sdk/RELEASE_NOTES`**: Release notes for the SDK.\n\n---\n\n## Development Workflow\n1. **Installation**: Run `./google-cloud-sdk/install.sh` to set up the SDK.\n2. **Authentication**: Use `gcloud auth login` to authenticate with Google Cloud.\n3. **Configuration**: Set the active project using `gcloud config set project PROJECT_ID`.\n4. **Usage**: Use `gcloud`, `gsutil`, or `bq` commands to interact with Google Cloud services.\n\n---\n\n## Testing\n- Unit and integration tests for `gsutil` are located in `google-cloud-sdk/platform/gsutil/gslib/tests/`.\n- Run tests using the Python test framework (e.g., `pytest`)."}}],"model":"mistral-large-latest","stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1493,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":30352,"output_tokens":992,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"cache_deleted_input_tokens":0,"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"msg_7646866ba0264ab4a53ff4988e036845","type":"assistant","uuid":"2acbaf4a-3f47-45ab-b531-04010013c086","timestamp":"2026-04-27T00:33:20.910Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/rayhanislamshuvro/Downloads","sessionId":"52bd585c-7130-43b2-b731-bfa1dca9e1f2","version":"unknown","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"sleepy-fluttering-blossom"}
